name: Ho et al., 'Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models', NeurIPS 2020

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (Ho et al., 2020)'. TOP region (full width): an inline-SVG horizontal Markov chain of SIX equal-width, equally-spaced state boxes 'x_0', 'x_1', '...', 'x_{t-1}', 'x_t', 'x_T' with forward arrows (top, labeled 'q(x_t | x_{t-1}) add noise') going left-to-right and reverse arrows (bottom, labeled 'p_theta(x_{t-1} | x_t) denoise') going right-to-left. BOTTOM region: TWO equal-width aligned panels - LEFT the equations 'q(x_t|x_{t-1}) = N(x_t; sqrt(1-beta_t) x_{t-1}, beta_t I)' and the simplified loss 'L = E[ || epsilon - epsilon_theta(x_t, t) ||^2 ]' with real subscripts/superscripts; RIGHT four bullets ('Forward: gradually add Gaussian noise', 'Reverse: learn to denoise step by step', 'Train epsilon-prediction network (U-Net)', 'Sample x_T ~ N(0, I), iterate to x_0'). Footnote at the bottom. ALSO add a third bottom block: an inline-SVG noise-schedule mini-chart of beta_t increasing linearly over t (labeled axes), making three equal-height aligned bottom blocks.
requirements: The six chain state boxes are equal width, equally spaced (uniform gaps), and share the same top and bottom edge
Forward arrows run left-to-right along the top and reverse arrows run right-to-left along the bottom, without their labels overlapping the boxes
Both equations render subscripts (x_t, x_{t-1}, beta_t) and the norm/superscript correctly
The two bottom panels are equal width and share the same top and bottom edge
The equations are fully inside the left panel with no horizontal overflow
All four right-hand bullets are present, each at most two lines, fully inside the panel
No element overlaps any other element on the slide
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and overlaps nothing
Every text element is at least 12px
The three bottom blocks (equations, bullets, noise-schedule chart) are equal height, aligned, with the chart's axes labeled and fully contained
task.paper: Ho et al., 'Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models', NeurIPS 2020

ss_html:
Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (Ho et al., 2020)

Mathematical Framework

q(x_t|x_{t-1}) = 𝒩(x_t; √(1-β_t) x_{t-1}, β_t 𝐈)
𝐋 = 𝔼[ ||ε - ε_θ(x_t, t)||² ]

Key Concepts

  • Forward: gradually add Gaussian noise
  • Reverse: learn to denoise step by step
  • Train ε-prediction network (U-Net)
  • Sample x_T ~ 𝒩(0, 𝐈), iterate to x₀

Noise Schedule β_t

Ho, J., Jain, A., & Abbeel, P. (2020). Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models. NeurIPS 2020.
rv_html:
Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (Ho et al., 2020)

Mathematical Formulation

q(xt|xt-1) = 𝒩(xt; √(1-βt) xt-1, βt I)
L = 𝔼[ || ε - εθ(xt, t) ||² ]

Key Concepts

  • Forward: gradually add Gaussian noise
  • Reverse: learn to denoise step by step
  • Train ε-prediction network (U-Net)
  • Sample xT ~ 𝒩(0, I), iterate to x0

Noise Schedule

t 0 T
Ho, J., Jain, A., & Abbeel, P. (2020). Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models. NeurIPS 2020.

Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models — Ho et al., 2020
📐 Mathematical Formulation
Forward Process
q(xt | xt−1) = 𝒩(xt ; √(1 − βt) · xt−1, βtI)
Reparameterization (Closed Form)
xt = √ᾱt · x0 + √(1 − ᾱt) · ε,   ε ~ 𝒩(0, I)
Simplified Training Objective
